title: In-vitro Transcription of T7 Cas9 mRNA for Drosophila Embryo Injections
author: Elizabeth Kim
materials: Qiagen QIAquick PCR purification kit Catalog # 28104
Qubit™ dsDNA Quantification Assay Kits Catalog number: Q32850

steps.title: Step 1
steps.instruction: Step 1: Midi Prep of plasmid - Qiagen Midi Prep Kit P508 T7-Cas9-nos-3'UTR

steps.title: Mix according to table and split...
steps.instruction: Mix according to table and split into 4 PCR tubes for 50 ul reaction for digestion %s %s H2O depends on DNA volume CutSmart Buffer 20 ul Enzyme EcoRI-HF 16 ul DNA ~ 16 ug TOTAL 200 ul Master Mix *Please note that the cut site EcoRI-HF is specific to the plasmid utilized here. Cut sites may vary depending on working construct.

steps.title: Incubate overnight at 37 o C
steps.instruction: Incubate overnight at 37 o C

steps.title: Prepare a culture of 100 mL to grow overnight
steps.instruction: Prepare a culture of 100 mL to grow overnight.

steps.title: Please refer to Qiagen’s midi prep...
steps.instruction: Please refer to Qiagen’s midi prep kit for full protocol instructions.

steps.title: Elute in 80 –ul of Elution Buffer or water
steps.instruction: Elute in 80 –ul of Elution Buffer or water. ( While the the protocol calls for 200 ul of Elution Buffer, using 80 -100 ul of Elution buffer will produce higher concentrated stock solution for linearization)

steps.title: While preparing plasmid does not require...
steps.instruction: While preparing plasmid does not require midi prep, it is useful in getting largeamounts with higher concentration. The T7 protocol requires a high concentrationof linearized product, around 900 ng to 1.2 ug. Higher concentrations oflinearized product results in higher concentrations of mRNA.

steps.title: Inactivate at 65 o C for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Inactivate at 65 o C for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Qiagen QIAquick PCR purification kit Catalog # 28104
steps.instruction: Qiagen QIAquick PCR purification kit Catalog # 28104

steps.title: Combine two of the Linearized reaction...
steps.instruction: Combine two of the Linearized reaction in order to follow the Qiagen protocol in 2 columns

steps.title: 100 uL rxn + 500 uL PB
steps.instruction: 100 uL rxn + 500 uL PB

steps.title: Elute in 12 - 15 uL total EB
steps.instruction: Elute in 12 - 15 uL total EB

steps.title: Quantify with Qubit™ dsDNA Quantification Assay...
steps.instruction: Quantify with Qubit™ dsDNA Quantification Assay Kits Catalog number: Q32850 If too concentrated add more water to column and spin again to collect and increasevolume. The goal of this step is to obtain a concentration of around 200 ng/ul or higher

steps.title: 1 kb ladder Lane
steps.instruction: 1 kb ladder Lane

steps.title: uncut plasmid (1ul of midiprep) Lane
steps.instruction: uncut plasmid (1ul of midiprep) Lane

steps.title: linearized plasmid pooled reaction 1 (1 ul) Lane
steps.instruction: linearized plasmid pooled reaction 1 (1 ul) Lane

steps.title: linearized plasmid pooled reaction 2 (1 ul)
steps.instruction: linearized plasmid pooled reaction 2 (1 ul)

steps.title: Following the manual of mMESSAGE mMACHINE®...
steps.instruction: Following the manual of mMESSAGE mMACHINE® T7 Ultra Kit (Page11-12) but in multiples of 4 reactions. Catalog # AM1345 from Ambion/Life Technologies

steps.title: Clean bench and wipe down area...
steps.instruction: Clean bench and wipe down area and pipettes with RNAse Away or RNAzap.

steps.title: Thaw, vortex and spin down all reagents
steps.instruction: Thaw, vortex and spin down all reagents: 10X Reaction Buffer and 2X NTP/CAP. Keep 2X NTP/ ARCA at 4 o C and 10X Reaction Buffer at room temperature during assembly

steps.title: Assemble the reactions into 1
steps.instruction: Assemble the reactions into 1.5 ul tube This is a master mix of 4 reactions (for different number of reactions see T7 protocol) %s %s Amount Component -varies Water 40 ul 2X NTP/ARCA 8 ul 10X Reaction Buffer ~3,600 – 4,800 ng Linear DNA 8 ul T7 Enzyme Mix 80 ul TOTAL VOLUME

steps.title: Mix and quick spin
steps.instruction: Mix and quick spin

steps.title: Split the reaction into 4 PCR...
steps.instruction: Split the reaction into 4 PCR tubes and incubate at 37 o C for 2 hours (DON’T CLOSE LID FOR INCUBATIONS : lid heating is necessary for iPCR but not for incubations)

steps.title: Degrade DNA by adding 1
steps.instruction: Degrade DNA by adding 1.0 ul of Turbo DNase I to each tube . Incubate at 37 o C for 15 minutes

steps.title: During 15 minute incubation, Mix A tailing reagent
steps.instruction: During 15 minute incubation, Mix A tailing reagent: This is a master mix of 4 reactions (for different number of reactions see T7 protocol) %s %s Amount Component 145 ul Nuclease Free water 80 ul 5X E-PAP Buffer 40 ul 25 mM MnCl2 40 ul ATP Solution * added an extra ul of water to provide room for equal distribution of A tailing Master Mix (from 144 ul to 145 ul water)

steps.title: Add 76 uL A-tailing reagents to each tube
steps.instruction: Add 76 uL A-tailing reagents to each tube.

steps.title: Bioanalyzer Step (optional) Take a 1
steps.instruction: Bioanalyzer Step (optional) Take a 1.5 uL aliquot from each PCR tube and pool into a separate PCR tube before adding A tailing to use as a tailing control. Pool 1-2 and 3-4 ”before A-tailing”.

steps.title: Add 4 μL E –PAP into...
steps.instruction: Add 4 μL E –PAP into each PCR tube mix gently (E-PAP enzyme facilitates A tailing)

steps.title: Incubate at 37°C for 45 min
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37°C for 45 min. Place on ice.

steps.title: Bioanalyzer Step (optional) Take a 1
steps.instruction: Bioanalyzer Step (optional) Take a 1.5 uL aliquot of each sample. Pool 1-2 and 3-4. after A-tailing”

steps.title: Stop the reaction and precipitate the...
steps.instruction: Stop the reaction and precipitate the RNA by adding 50 μL LiCl Precipitation Solution.

steps.title: Pool 1-2 and 3-4 into two separate 1
steps.instruction: Pool 1-2 and 3-4 into two separate 1.5 ml eppendorf tube to avoid spilling. Mix thoroughly.

steps.title: Chill O/N at –20°C
steps.instruction: Chill O/N at –20°C.

steps.title: Pre-chill a centrifuge to 2 -...
steps.instruction: Pre-chill a centrifuge to 2 - 4°C and Centrifuge at maximum speed for 30 to 45 minutes to pellet the RNA.

steps.title: During centrifugation, make fresh 2 mL...
steps.instruction: During centrifugation, make fresh 2 mL of 70% Ethanol using 200 proof ACS Ethanol

steps.title: After centrifugation, leave one reaction in...
steps.instruction: After centrifugation, leave one reaction in chilled centrifuge at 2-4°C while workingon other reaction for steps 17-21.

steps.title: After centrifugation, check for presence of small white pellet
steps.instruction: After centrifugation, check for presence of small white pellet. Should be a sizable amount. Approximately this big o or larger. Anything smaller will probably yield small concentrations

steps.title: Remove the supernatant without disturbing pellet
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant without disturbing pellet.

steps.title: Wash the pellet with the prepared...
steps.instruction: Wash the pellet with the prepared ~1 mL 70% ethanol and re-centrifuge.

steps.title: Remove the 70% ethanol
steps.instruction: Remove the 70% ethanol

steps.title: Resuspend the RNA in 100 uL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the RNA in 100 uL of Elution Solution (from MEGAclear™ Transcription Clean-Up Kit) When resuspend either vortex or pipette up and down until you can no longer see pellet. Please note that the white pellet will turn translucent as it resuspends. It will be easier to see translucent pellet dissolve completely by pipetting up and down.

steps.title: MEGAclear™ Transcription Clean-Up Kit Catalog # AM1908 from Invitrogen
steps.instruction: MEGAclear™ Transcription Clean-Up Kit Catalog # AM1908 from Invitrogen

steps.title: Add 350 ul Binding Solution to...
steps.instruction: Add 350 ul Binding Solution to the 100 ul reaction and mix well

steps.title: Add 250 ul 100% ethanol and mix well
steps.instruction: Add 250 ul 100% ethanol and mix well.

steps.title: Apply the sample (700ul) in the...
steps.instruction: Apply the sample (700ul) in the Spin column and spin for 15s. Discard the flow-through.

steps.title: Wash the column with 2 x...
steps.instruction: Wash the column with 2 x 500 μL Wash Solution and spin for 15s. Discard the flow-through

steps.title: Spin for an extra minute to...
steps.instruction: Spin for an extra minute to get rid of any residual wash solution

steps.title: Transfer columns/tubes to new tubes
steps.instruction: Transfer columns/tubes to new tubes.

steps.title: Apply 20 of nuclease free water
steps.instruction: Apply 20 of nuclease free water. Close the tube and incubate at 65–70°C for 5–10 min, spin for 1min. *Make sure to elute with water and not elution buffer as this final product will be used as part of the injection solution for embryo injection.

steps.title: Repeat with a second 20 μL...
steps.instruction: Repeat with a second 20 μL of Elution Solution with another incubation period of 65 – 70 °C foranother 5 minutes.

steps.title: Qubit quantify using Qubit™ RNA Broad...
steps.instruction: Qubit quantify using Qubit™ RNA Broad Range (BR) Assay Kit Catalog # Q10210 Typically concentrations for injections should be higher than 500 ng/ul so concentration amounts can be adjusted for injection solutions

steps.title: Bioanalyzer Step (optional) Take a 3 uL aliquot
steps.instruction: Bioanalyzer Step (optional) Take a 3 uL aliquot.  “after A-tailing and cleaning (purify)” then take all three sample aliquots and the RNA ladder (~ 2 ul) and incubate at 90 °C for 2 minutes before loading on chip.

steps.title: Before A-tailing Lane
steps.instruction: Before A-tailing Lane

steps.title: After A-tailing Sample
steps.instruction: After A-tailing Sample

steps.title: Before A-tailing Sample
steps.instruction: Before A-tailing Sample

steps.title: After A-tailing Comments
steps.instruction: After A-tailing Comments: The product looks good and final RNA concentration is 1.2 ug/μl, volume is 100 uL.

steps.title: Note that any linearized product not...
steps.instruction: Note that any linearized product not used in the transcription process may be stored at 4 o C for several months. If using a previously linearized DNA, make sure to run a second gel to confirm DNA remains linearized.
